title: bartlettina solavegana
intro: Bartlettina solavegana is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the "Mexican mint plant" due to its mint-like fragrance. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Bartlettina solavegana include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and colds, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol.
